title: January 2026 Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: March 5, 2026
TO: Mayor and City Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services
SUBJECT: January 2026 Clean and Safe Team Homeless Actions and Outcomes
The City of Reno continues to address homeless-related concerns and provide outreach to the
unsheltered community within city limits through the Clean and Safe Team. The Clean and Safe Team interdepartmental team plays a critical role in addressing the multifaceted challenges and
community impacts associated with homelessness, while coordinating multiple concurrent efforts
and programs to ensure a comprehensive, citywide response.
Clean and Safe Team - Departments
• Licensing and Code Enforcement
• Community Engagement and
Services – Clean and Safe, Reno
Direct
• City Manager's Office - Security
• Housing and Neighborhood
Development
• Maintenance and Operations
• Parks and Recreation
• Reno Police – Community Action
and Outreach (CAO), Mobile
Engagement Team (MET), Parking
Enforcement
The following is an overview of January 2026 data, actions, outcomes, and updates from the
Clean and Safe Team:
January 2026 Key Highlights
• 12 Community Engagement and
Outreach Efforts
• 25 Outreach Interactions
• 6 Individuals into Transitional
Housing
• 151 Citations
• 73 Arrests

• 2 Large Encampment Clean-Ups
• 17 Smaller Encampment Clean-Ups
• 161 Yards of Trash Collected
• 51 Occupied Vehicles Posted
• 10 Occupied Vehicles Towed
• 3 Park Exclusions
• 37 Code Enforcement Cases
• 96 Allied Security Site Visit
January Updates
• 2026 Point-in-Time (PIT) Count: Clean and Safe, Reno Police Department, and Park
and River Rangers participated in the 2026 PIT Count on January 29. Most staff worked
4:00–9:00am in high-concentration inner-city zones. The Clean and Safe Outreach
Coordinator joined the Tier 3 team, focusing on larger outer-city areas, including Reno
outskirts and Sun Valley.
• Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program: In January, Clean and Safe
Outreach facilitated 6 individuals into the Motel Assistance program. The 6 individuals are
currently awaiting longer term housing.
• Camera Mobile Trailers: Clean and Safe's Mobile Camera Trailer Pilot Program is
ongoing. The mobile trailers continue to keep previously chronic areas clear and provide
an extra tool for staff to monitor. The mobile trailers are currently installed at Spokane Street,
along the railroad and Galletti Way, along the railroad.
• Railroad Memorandum of Understanding (MOU): In December 2025, the City of
Reno and Karma Box Project established a Memorandum of Understanding (MOU) to
actively monitor and complete proactive maintenance services along the railroad corridor
from Record Street to Sutro Street. Over the last couple of months, the MOU between the City of Reno and Karma Box Project has been
effective in maintaining the railroad corridor from Record Street to Sutro Street and ensuring the railroad corridor from Record Street to Sutro Street remains clear and clean.
• Waste Management Donation: On January 14, Council approved a $53,141 donation
from Waste Management to support Clean and Safe maintenance efforts citywide.
• Karma Box Project Work Crew: The Karma Box Project Work Crew program
relaunched, helping address debris and maintain public spaces citywide. Deployment is
coordinated by Clean and Safe.
• City of Reno and Nevada Department of Transportation Interlocal Agreement: On
January 28, Council approved an interlocal agreement between the City of Reno and
NDOT for maintenance and outreach efforts on NDOT rights-of-way in Reno city limits.
Community Engagement and Partnerships
Multiple departments partake in standing or topic specific meetings with regional partners to
help address homelessness, identify support, and advance collaborative, solution-oriented
initiatives. January highlights are as follows:

Connect - City of

and Downtown Reno

Community Engagement
and Services
Clean and Safe, Cares Campus, and
Downtown Reno Partnership staff met to
discuss homeless-related efforts across all

and the group discussed how to better
collaborate to address shared concerns.

Victorian 1 0
This client was experiencing unsheltered
homelessness in Reno and was referred by the
City of Reno's Housing and Neighborhood Development team to Clean and Safe for the
Motel Assistance Program, while the client was
awaiting a move-in date at Silverada Manor.
The client is blind and previously completed a
training program in Sparks to learn essential
skills, such as navigation, and prefers to stay
in Sparks as the client is familiar with the
surrounding area.

Arrived on site with the Sparks HOPE Team
to meet with the client to discuss the client's housing
plan. The client is currently on the Reno Housing Authority waitlist and is waiting for the client's move-in date
at Silverada Manor.

Enforcement and Compliance
Reno Police Department
Reno Police Department's Community Action and Outreach (CAO) and Mobile Engagement
Team (MET) outcomes:
• Unsheltered Contacts: 99
• Posted Occupied Vehicles: 51
• Vehicles Towed: 10
• Citations: 135
• Felony Arrests: 29
• Misdemeanor Arrests: 44
Park and River Rangers
In January, the Park and River Rangers conducted 739 site-visits. The Park and River Rangers
made 118 interactions with unsheltered individuals, which included issuing warnings, citations,
or providing service referrals.
Park and River Rangers enforcement efforts in City Parks and along the Truckee River:
• Citations issued: 16
o 14 citations issued for sitting,
lying, sleeping, or camping
on a public place
• Park exclusions issued: 3
o 2 Idlewild Park
o 1 Brodhead Park

• Code Enforcement Cases: 37

• Warning of Violation: 11
• First Administrative Citation: 2
• Second Administrative Citation: 0
• Third Administrative Citation: 2
Allied Universal Security
There are two Allied Universal Security officers dedicated to Clean and Safe, patrolling chronic
hotspots, monitoring recent cleanup efforts, and issuing written notices to encampments in public
spaces. Notices support expediting enforcement efforts.

Large Clean-Up Operations
Large clean-up operations include 3 or more established encampments and/or large amounts of
debris, which require the Clean and Safe provider's services.
West 4th Street and McCarran Blvd, along Truckee River and Sage Street and Railroad
• Number of Notices Issued: 4

o West 4th Street and McCarran Blvd, along Truckee River and Sage Street and Railroad area to be spot cleaned by Karma Box

Union Pacific Clean-Up
The City of Reno has monthly coordinated clean-up efforts with Union Pacific. Areas addressed in
January includes the ReTrac Corridor from Dickerson Road to Commercial Row, Dickerson
Road along the train tracks and West Fourth Street from Summit Ridge Drive to Stagg Lane,
along the train tracks.

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
fields.tldr: The Clean and Safe Team's January 2026 report details interdepartmental homeless outreach, enforcement, and maintenance efforts across Reno. Key outcomes include 25 outreach interactions, 6 individuals placed into transitional housing, 73 arrests, 151 citations, and 161 yards of trash collected across 22 cleanup operations. Notable updates include a $53,141 Waste Management donation approved by Council, a new interlocal agreement with NDOT, and the relaunch of the Karma Box Project Work Crew. Reno Direct service requests rose 20% month-over-month, driven largely by a 29% increase in occupied vehicle nuisance requests.
fields.key_stats: 25 outreach interactions; 6 individuals into transitional housing; 3 shelter arrangements; 151 citations (135 RPD, 16 Park/River Rangers); 73 arrests (29 felony, 44 misdemeanor); 99 unsheltered contacts by RPD; 51 occupied vehicles posted; 10 vehicles towed; 3 park exclusions; 37 code enforcement cases; 96 Allied Universal Security site visits; 739 Park and River Ranger site visits; 118 interactions with unsheltered individuals by rangers; 22 cleanups; 161 yards of trash collected; 325 total Reno Direct service requests (20% increase from December's 270); 2 large encampment clean-ups; 17 smaller encampment clean-ups
fields.key_decisions: January 14: Council approved a $53,141 donation from Waste Management to support Clean and Safe maintenance efforts citywide. January 28: Council approved an interlocal agreement between the City of Reno and NDOT for maintenance and outreach on NDOT rights-of-way within Reno city limits.
fields.action_items: Ongoing deployment of Mobile Camera Trailer Pilot Program at Spokane Street and Galletti Way along the railroad; continued MOU maintenance of railroad corridor from Record Street to Sutro Street with Karma Box Project; monthly coordinated Union Pacific clean-up efforts; coordination of Karma Box Project Work Crew deployment citywide; continued facilitation of individuals into Reno Housing Authority Motel Assistance Program; follow-up on family reunification case involving partner in Redding, California; ongoing City of Reno and Cares Campus Collaboration meetings to transition shelter clients with income into permanent housing

departments: Reno Police Department – Community Action and Outreach (CAO)
Reno Police Department – Mobile Engagement Team (MET)

people: Cynthia Esparza, Director, Community Engagement and Services
Kaytlin Canner, Clean and Safe Manager
